name: Zafra and Los Santos de Maimona Loop
name_original: De Zafra a Los Santos de Maimona — circular por Tierra de Barros
description: Cycle a moderate 12.0-mile (19.4 km) circular route from Zafra to Los Santos de Maimona, exploring Extremadura's agricultural landscapes.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Zafra traverse a landscape characterized by extensive dehesas, gently rolling hills, and the distinct "island mountain" of Sierra Grande de Hornachos. The region features a mix of holm oak and cork oak forests, rocky outcrops, and Mediterranean scrubland. Zafra is also a key stop on the historic Vía de la Plata, offering routes that blend ancient paths with scenic countryside. These varied terrains provide diverse experiences for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Zafra The most popular touring cycling route is Zafra and Los Santos de Maimona Loop, a 12.0 miles (19.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects two historic towns through the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is From Zafra to Puebla de Sancho Pérez — circular by the EV1, a moderate 33.8 miles (54.4 km) path. This circular route follows part of the European cycle network, offering a blend of rural scenery and well-defined paths. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Los Santos de Maimona – Zafra Old Town loop from Zafra Feria, a 25.0 miles (40.2 km) trail leading through Zafra's historic areas and offering views towards Los Santos de Maimona, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Zafra is defined by dehesas, rolling hills, and the Sierra Grande de Hornachos. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Zafra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 5 reviews. More than 140 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Zafra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Zafra?
A: Touring cycling routes around Zafra primarily feature extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, which are traditional agroforestry systems dominated by holm oaks and cork oaks, and gently rolling hills. You'll also encounter the distinct 'island mountain' of Sierra Grande de Hornachos, characterized by rocky outcrops and Mediterranean scrubland. The region offers a blend of quiet rural roads, olive groves, and cork oak forests, providing a varied and scenic backdrop for your rides.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Zafra area?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Zafra, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include options for easy, moderate, and difficult rides, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: What are the best times of year to go touring cycling in Zafra?
A: The most favorable times for touring cycling in Zafra are spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these periods, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, allowing you to avoid the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Zafra?
A: While the guide highlights routes with varying difficulties, some routes around Zafra can be challenging with unpaved segments. For a moderate option that connects two historic towns through the countryside, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022041896/zafra-and-los-santos-de-maimona-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafra and Los Santos de Maimona Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Always check the route details for surface type and elevation to ensure it suits your family's abilities.

Q: What historical sites or natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes in Zafra?
A: Zafra is rich in history and natural beauty. You can cycle past significant landmarks such as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parador Nacional Duques de Feria&lt;/a&gt;, a 15th-century palace, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6458193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Grande&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the unique landscapes of the Sierra Grande de Hornachos, a protected area with rocky outcrops and dense forests. The historic Vía de la Plata also runs through the region, offering glimpses of ancient Roman remains.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Zafra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Zafra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 5 reviews. Over 140 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the blend of rural scenery and well-defined paths, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353828071/from-zafra-to-puebla-de-sancho-perez-circular-by-the-ev1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Zafra to Puebla de Sancho Pérez — circular by the EV1&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Zafra?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353828071/from-zafra-to-puebla-de-sancho-perez-circular-by-the-ev1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Zafra to Puebla de Sancho Pérez — circular by the EV1&lt;/a&gt;, which follows part of the European cycle network. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Los Santos de Maimona – Zafra Old Town loop from Zafra Feria&lt;/a&gt;, offering views towards Los Santos de Maimona and passing through Zafra's historic areas.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs along the touring cycling routes in Zafra?
A: Zafra's central Plaza Grande and the older Plaza Chica are vibrant hubs with numerous restaurants and bars, perfect for a break before or after your ride. While specific cafes directly on rural routes are not detailed, many routes will pass through or near smaller towns where you can find refreshments. The historic town center of Zafra itself is a great place to refuel.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Zafra?
A: The routes around Zafra offer a range of difficulty levels. You'll find easy options, but the majority are moderate, and there are also challenging routes, with 10 out of 25 routes classified as difficult. These more challenging routes can include unpaved and difficult segments, requiring good to very good fitness.

Q: Is it possible to combine touring cycling with exploring Zafra's historic town?
A: Absolutely. Zafra's historic town center, recognized as a National Heritage Site, is easily accessible and offers a rich cultural experience. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafra Old Town – El Capricho de Cotrina loop from Zafra Feria&lt;/a&gt; specifically lead through Zafra's historic areas, allowing you to seamlessly blend your ride with exploring landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3804712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Tower of Zafra&lt;/a&gt; and the ancient gateways.

Q: Are there any specific natural protected areas near Zafra that I can cycle through?
A: Yes, the Sierra Grande de Hornachos, located a short distance from Zafra, is a protected area designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA) and a Special Conservation Zone. While specific cycling routes directly through its core are not detailed, the broader region's touring cycling routes traverse landscapes influenced by this 'island mountain,' offering views of its rocky outcrops and diverse Mediterranean scrubland.
tips: It is worth deviating from the national highway and approaching the traditional layout of the Roman road to get to know Zafra. Its location in the transit of travelers and merchandise made it a nerve center of commerce in the Middle Ages, and it is not in vain that it continues to have a commercial character due to its tradition as a trade fair. The remains of the old medieval wall attest to its stately importance and its interior architecture in the style of the white villages of neighboring Andalusia make it worth a stop.

The tower of the convent of San Francisco is the only remains of what was the Franciscan convent of San Benito. It was located outside the city of Zafra, although it is currently within the urban area.
The convent was founded and built in the last third of the 15th century by the second Count of Feria, Gomes II Suárez de Figueroa, and by his mother, Countess María Manuel. Starting in 1565, the cloister and bedrooms were enlarged with the aim of housing a greater number of religious.
After a gentle climb leaving Zafra behind, you come across an immense pine forest where a steep descent begins to Los Santos de Maimona. Let yourself go and enjoy the views!

The mason Francisco González Gragera created this house starting in 1988 for his little daughter, who dreamed of a country house that was different from everything normal, like in a fairy tale, like a princess castle.
The hermitage of Belén de Zafra was built in the 18th century.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 19.4 kilometers long and generally takes around 1 hour and 31 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the 'From Zafra to Los Santos de Maimona' cycling route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features paved surfaces, making it well-suited for touring cycling. However, as a moderate route, you might encounter some unpaved or more challenging segments. Expect to cycle on wide tracks through fields of cereals and olive trees, characteristic of the agricultural landscape of Tierra de Barros.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate,' this route requires a good level of fitness. While much of it is paved, the inclusion of potential unpaved sections and an ascent to the Alto de San Cristóbal means it's not ideal for absolute beginners. Cyclists with some experience will find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the lush landscapes of Extremadura, characterized by rolling hills and extensive olive groves. The route connects the historic towns of Zafra and Los Santos de Maimona, offering a scenic backdrop of cultivated fields and traditional Spanish countryside.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the town of Zafra, home to the San Francisco Tower of Zafra and the Ermita de Belén de Zafra. You can also enjoy a View of Los Santos de Maimona and see the unique El Capricho de Cotrina.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Tierra de Barros region, like much of Extremadura, can get very hot in summer. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with comfortable weather and beautiful landscapes.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Zafra, you can begin your ride from various points within the town. Zafra offers parking options, particularly near its historic center, making it convenient to access the trail.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through agricultural areas and towns, there isn't specific information regarding dog-friendliness for cycling this particular trail. If you plan to bring a dog, it's advisable to keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, and ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the 'From Zafra to Los Santos de Maimona — circular through Tierra de Barros' route. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You'll find that it intersects with parts of the Mtb Vía de la Plata, the Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida, and the Camino de Santiago del Sur.

faqs.question: What should I pack for cycling this moderate route?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and duration, it's recommended to pack essentials like water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your bicycle, and appropriate cycling attire. Sun protection (sunscreen, hat) is also advisable, especially during warmer months. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

waypoints.name: View of Los Santos de Maimona
